data.title: Pinterest Raises Q1 Guidance Following tvScientific Deal
data.description:
Pinterest, Inc. (NYSE:PINS) shares are trading higher after the company raised first-quarter 2026 sales guidance following the completion of the previously announced acquisition of tvScientific, Inc.

In December 2025, Pinterest agreed to buy tvScientific, expanding its AI-powered performance ad offerings to connected TV. The company plans to integrate tvScientific’s outcome-based CTV platform into its performance ads suite.

Outlook Raised

The company increased its first-quarter outlook to reflect a partial-quarter contribution from the acquisition.

The company increased the sales guidance from $951 million-$971 million to $958 million-$978 million (vs. the outlook of $980.12 million).

Also, the company now sees adjusted EBITDA of $163 million-$183 million vs. $166 million-$186 million earlier.

Recent Earnings Release

This month, the idea discovery platform company delivered fourth-quarter revenue of $1.32 billion, missing analyst estimates of $1.33 billion, and adjusted earnings of 67 cents per share for the quarter, missing analyst estimates of 69 cents per share.

Total revenue was up 14% on a year-over-year basis as Pinterest’s global monthly active users increased 12% year-over-year to 619 million. Average revenue per user in the third quarter was $2.16 globally and $9.41 in the U.S. and Canada.

PINS Price Action: Pinterest shares were up 5.71% at $16.39 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Fusion Fuel Expands Clean Energy Reach Via Royal Uranium Acquisition
data.description:
Fusion Fuel Green PLC (NASDAQ:HTOO) shares are down on Wednesday. The company announced a significant acquisition aimed at enhancing its position in the clean energy sector.

Details

Fusion Fuel has entered into a definitive Share Exchange Agreement to acquire a controlling interest in Royal Uranium Inc., which includes a diversified portfolio of uranium royalties.

In particular, Fusion Fuel will acquire 75–100% of Royal Uranium, issuing up to 3.75 million Fusion Fuel shares to Royal Uranium's shareholders as consideration.

This acquisition is expected to provide Fusion Fuel with exposure to sixteen uranium and three natural gas royalties, positioning the company to benefit from the growing demand for clean energy sources as global electrification and AI infrastructure expand.

Notably, the portfolio provides diversified uranium and producing Alberta natural gas royalties, offering near-term cash flow and exposure to long-term energy demand while avoiding typical mining risks.

The deal is anticipated to close in the first half of 2026.

By securing this portfolio, Fusion Fuel aims to capitalize on the structural growth in uranium demand, which is forecasted to increase significantly through 2040, while supply growth lags behind.

The acquisition of uranium and natural gas royalties is strategically significant as it aligns with the growing demand for clean energy sources.

Notably, the company said that global data center power demand is expected to surge by over 160% by 2030, while uranium demand is projected to outpace supply through 2040, creating a sustained structural deficit.

Technical Analysis

The broader market experienced slight gains on the previous trading day, with the S&P 500 closing up 0.30% and the Nasdaq rising 0.41%. Despite this positive backdrop, Fusion Fuel's shares are underperforming, suggesting that company-specific factors may be influencing the stock's movement.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The stock is currently trading 10.4% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 16.0% below its 50-day SMA, indicating bearish momentum in the short term.

Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 77.06%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing challenges for the company.

The RSI is at 45.56, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that traders should remain cautious.

  • Key Resistance: $4.50
  • Key Support: $3.50

HTOO Price Action: Fusion Fuel Green shares were down 3.43% at $3.38 at the time of publication on Wednesday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $2.92, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Non-Union Project Managers Wanted The Same Wage As Union Engineers. Dave Ramsey Replied, 'I'm Not Trying To Bust A Union, But…'
data.description:
Pay gaps inside companies can stir up tension fast, especially when managers oversee employees who earn more than they do. That was the situation one caller brought to the “EntreLeadership” podcast in August.

AJ, a manager at an electrical engineering company in Minnesota, oversees about 10 staff members. Half are union field engineers. The other half are non-union project managers. The issue? The project managers now earn less than the union engineers they manage.

Market Sets The Pay

AJ explained that historically, office staff made more than the field crews. “That is now flipped,” he said. Project managers assign projects and distribute workload, while union field engineers handle on-site technical work.

Don't Miss:

Personal finance expert and entrepreneur Dave Ramsey cut straight to the core of the issue. “They do different jobs,” he said. “It doesn’t matter if they’re union or not. It’s just they get paid different because they do different jobs.”

In other words, this is not about fairness. It is about role and market value.

Ramsey compared it to any other company. A high-performing salesperson who brings in millions will not earn the same as an entry-level accounting employee. “It’s apples to oranges,” he said.

AJ pressed further, saying that project managers now earn less than the engineers they supervise. Ramsey's response stayed consistent. If someone applied fresh today, they would apply for one role or the other. Each role pays what the market says it pays.

Trending: Own the Characters, Not Just the Content: Inside a Fast-Growing Pre-IPO IP Company

He also challenged AJ to look at the broader labor market. Could project managers leave and double their income somewhere else? No. Could union engineers leave and make the same pay at a non-union competitor? Also no.

That told Ramsey everything he needed to know.

“It’s what the marketplace pays,” he said. “Union electrical engineers make this and we have union electrical engineers. Project managers make this and this is what you make.”

Ramsey questioned the business logic of paying more for union labor if competitors are not, saying, “I’m not trying to bust a union, but from a business perspective, why am I paying more and not be union and pay less, and that’s what my competitor is doing?”  

To Ramsey, the real question was how AJ should explain the difference to his team.

Ramsey's advice was to be clear and confident. “Look, that position pays this, this position pays that,” he said. “The positions just pay different. It’s not a personal thing.”

If both roles are paid at or above market, there is no grievance to fix. “You chose project manager,” Ramsey said. “This is what you chose.”

The broader lesson applies beyond engineering firms. Compensation is shaped by supply, demand and skill specialization. Titles and reporting lines do not automatically determine salary.

In AJ's case, Ramsey's message was to simply explain the market reality and move forward. If the pay matches the market, the structure is working as designed.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Uber Stocks Climb As Ride-Hailing Giant Pours $100 Million Into New Robotaxi Charging Hubs
data.description:
Uber Technologies Inc (NYSE:UBER) is investing over $100 million to build charging infrastructure to support its autonomous fleet, signaling a significant push to scale its self-driving services.

Uber's $100 Million Charging Initiative

The company said the buildout will focus on high-speed charging at locations tied to daily fleet management and other stop-in points in key markets.

The effort includes DC fast chargers at Uber's autonomous depots and additional charging "pit stops" across priority cities, Reuters reported on Wednesday.

The first wave is set to start in the U.S., beginning with the Bay Area, Los Angeles and Dallas, before expanding to additional locations.

Uber has been leaning harder into autonomy, with the company describing self-driving as a central part of its strategy.

The Reuters report said Uber has more than 20 partnerships worldwide spanning autonomous trucking, deliveries and ride-hailing.

The company also pointed to its existing robotaxi availability on the Uber app in four U.S. cities, along with Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Riyadh.

Partnerships Fuel Expansion of Charging Network

Alongside building its own sites, Uber is working with charging-network operators in multiple regions using what it called "utilization guarantee agreements."

Reuters said those arrangements include EVgo in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco and Boston, plus Electra in Paris and Madrid, and Hubber and Ionity in London.

The rollout is designed to support autonomous fleet operations as Uber tries to scale service and defend its share against rivals, including Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA).

Q4 2025 Financial Results

Following its fiscal fourth-quarter 2025 earnings report, Uber's stock gained.

The ride-hailing giant reported a 20% year-over-year revenue growth, reaching $14.37 billion, exceeding analyst expectations of $14.32 billion.

However, its adjusted EPS of 71 cents missed the consensus estimate of 80 cents.

Revenue from Mobility increased by 19% to $8.20 billion, Delivery grew 30% to $4.89 billion, and Freight remained flat at $1.27 billion.

Gross Bookings rose 22% to $54.14 billion, with Mobility growing by 20%, Delivery by 26%, and Freight declining by 1%.

The company also saw 22% growth in trips, totaling 3.75 billion, and 18% growth in Monthly Active Platform Consumers, reaching 202 million.

Looking ahead, Uber expects first-quarter 2026 gross bookings between $52.0 billion and $53.5 billion, indicating 17%-21% growth, and adjusted EBITDA between $2.37 billion and $2.47 billion.

The company anticipates quarterly adjusted EPS of 65 cents to 72 cents, below the consensus estimate of 75 cents.

UBER Price Action: Uber Technologies shares were up 3.31% at $72.86 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by JHVEPhoto via Shutterstock

data.title: Starfighters Space (FJET) Shares Soar As Space Stocks Rebound
data.description:
Starfighters Space Inc (NYSE:FJET) shares are trading sharply higher Wednesday. The move caps an 87% gain in the past week after the stock tumbled over 50% to start 2026.

Wednesday’s rally appears driven less by headlines and more by the stock finding technical support, potentially sparking short-covering and dip-buying as traders step back in.

  • Starfighters Space stock is among today’s top performers. Why is FJET stock up today?

Small Caps Lead Risk-On Rally

FJET's rebound is unfolding alongside firmer risk sentiment. The iShares Russell 2000 ETF is up around 1% Wednesday afternoon, signaling renewed appetite for small-cap and speculative growth names that were under pressure earlier in the month.

SpaceX IPO Chatter Weighed

Recently, reports that SpaceX may pursue a blockbuster IPO valued near $1.5 trillion pressured listed space stocks, as investors reserved capital for a potential listing and reassessed valuations across the group.

Going into Wednesday’s session, the space sector had seen steep one-month declines in peers such as AST SpaceMobile, Rocket Lab and Virgin Galactic.

Focus Returns To FJET Story

With space stocks surging Wednesday afternoon, FJET's rebound suggests traders are betting the worst of the recent shakeout may be over and that the name has found near-term support.

Commercial Aerospace Leader At Kennedy

Starfighters Space is a commercial aerospace company based at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The company operates supersonic aircraft capable of sustained Mach 2 flight and configured to carry payloads to high altitudes for air launch to space.

Its activities include air launch operations, pilot and space flight training, support research, hypersonic testing and other aerospace development programs.

The company provides commercial space services and related aerospace capabilities, making it a significant player in the evolving space economy.

Strong Short-Term Momentum Signals

Currently, the stock is trading 28.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased 10.82%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting a solid upward trend.

The RSI is at 49.25, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. Meanwhile, MACD is at -1.3558, below its signal line at -1.3140, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is performing well today, there may be underlying bearish signals to watch.

  • Key Support: $9.00

FJET Stock Climbs Wednesday

FJET Price Action: Starfighters Space shares were up 34.45% at $9.60 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
